problem_addressed: The paper targets the challenge of predicting scalar coupling constants between atom pairs in molecules, a critical task in computational chemistry and drug discovery.
core_claim: Machine learning agents can be effectively evaluated on the complex task of predicting molecular properties from structural data using a standardized benchmark framework.
key_contributions: Provides a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating ML agents on molecular property prediction
Offers a structured evaluation framework with Log MAE metric averaged across coupling types
Releases a rich dataset with multiple molecular features including XYZ coordinates, dipole moments, and magnetic shielding tensors
novelty: The novelty lies in the comprehensive evaluation framework and the structured approach to benchmarking ML agents on molecular property prediction with equal weighting across different coupling types.
method_summary: The competition involves predicting scalar coupling constants using various ML approaches, with evaluation based on the Log of Mean Absolute Error calculated for each coupling type and averaged across types.
method_components: Feature extraction from molecular structures (XYZ files)
Utilization of additional molecular properties (dipole moments, magnetic shielding tensors, etc.)
Evaluation using Log MAE with a floor of 1e-9 for numerical stability
key_innovation: The structured evaluation framework that averages Log MAE across different scalar coupling types, ensuring equal weight for each type through the mathematical formulation: score = (1/T) Σ log(1/n_t Σ |y_i - ŷ_i|)
assumptions: Training and test splits are by molecule, ensuring no molecule appears in both sets
Scalar coupling constants can be predicted from the provided molecular features
Log MAE is an appropriate metric for evaluating prediction quality
theoretical_grounding: The paper provides the mathematical formulation for the evaluation metric and specifies that the MAE has a floor of 1e-9, making the best possible score approximately -20.7232 for perfect predictions.
title: MLE-bench: Evaluating Machine Learning Agents on Machine Learning Engineering
